repoAnalysis.purpose: COS.Lending.Selling.UI is a React-based frontend application for managing loan selling operations at Cross River Bank. It provides interfaces for viewing, managing, and processing loans for sale, including loan details, filtering, and loan history functionality.
businessFeatures: Loan management and tracking with comprehensive loan details view
Loan history tracking and querying through an AI-powered chat interface
Long-term held for sale (LTHFS) loan management
Loan filtering, sorting, and searching capabilities

apiIntegrations.name: Selling API
apiIntegrations.purpose: Main backend API for loan selling operations

apiIntegrations.name: AI API
apiIntegrations.purpose: AI-powered loan history and information retrieval

dataEntities.name: LoanDetails
fields: Extended loan information including dates, statuses, and financial details

dataEntities.name: LTHFS (Long-term Held For Sale)
fields: Loan data for long-term held for sale items

keyFiles.description: Main application entry point that sets up routing and global UI structure
AI-powered chat interface for querying loan history with SQL execution capabilities
Component that displays detailed loan information in a side drawer
Main loans table component that handles displaying and interacting with loan data
Environment-specific configuration for development environment including API endpoints and role definitions
API endpoints definitions for backend communication
Role-based permission configurations for feature access
Type definitions for loan-related data structures
Utilities for Cypress testing including authentication and table verification

findings.description: The application uses CRB's internal authentication system with different roles for internal and external users, integrated through the Identity Provider at idptest.crbcos.com (non-prod) and idp.crbcos.com (prod)
Loan history component utilizes a streaming API that executes SQL queries against the database and returns results to the UI
Application includes different views for internal CRB users versus external partners (MPL, Investors, and Issuing Banks) with role-based access controls
